rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe HS pain crisis needs urgent same-day in-person assessment for analgesia (± intralesional steroid) and to start anti-inflammatory antibiotics, but without systemic infection — T3, escalating for superimposed cellulitis/sepsis.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rapidly spreading hot redness down the arm/chest, high fever >38.5°C with rigors, or intolerable pain
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals superimposed cellulitis/sepsis needing systemic antibiotics ± admission.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads rapidly down your arm or chest, you get a high fever with shakes, or the pain becomes unbearable — go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide psychosocial support/referral and ensure access to urgent review and dermatology follow-up.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a hidradenitis flare — start the anti-inflammatory antibiotic and pain relief, use absorbent dressings and loose clothing, and get an in-person review today for pain and a possible steroid injection. A dermatology referral is important. Watch for spreading redness or high fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP for analgesia/assessment; ED for spreading cellulitis/sepsis; dermatology referral
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for pain and to start treatment; ED for spreading infection or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document an HS (Hurley II/III) flare, anti-inflammatory antibiotics + analgesia commenced, avoid blind I&D, smoking cessation, and urgent dermatology referral.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing a severe HS flare with no analgesia plan or cellulitis safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A superimposed cellulitis/sepsis is missed, or the severe pain crisis is left unmanaged.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays analgesia and anti-inflammatory treatment of a severe flare.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency ambulance for an HS flare without systemic infection.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate the extent or assess a dominant fluctuant nodule remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as an HS flare with analgesia + anti-inflammatory antibiotics + dressings + referral, arrange in-person review for any dominant nodule, and safety-net for cellulitis/sepsis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Advising blind I&D, or treating purely as a simple infection.
